value.Title: University of Houston : AI Model Predicts Battery Decay to Make Renewable Energy More Reliable

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK3-RNV3-S0W1-23CS-00000-00University of Houston: AI Model Predicts Battery Decay to Make Renewable Energy More Reliable2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:13:28ZLexisNexisUniversity of Houston: AI Model Predicts Battery Decay to Make Renewable Energy More ReliableTargeted News ServiceHOUSTON, Texas

The University of Houston issued the following news:

* * *

New AI Model Predicts Battery Decay to Make Renewable Energy More Reliable

University of Houston Researchers Develop AI Model That Predicts Battery Aging, Preventing Grid Failures

By J.J. Adams, 713-743-8960, jadams23@central.uh.edu

As the global demand for reliable electricity grows, University of Houston researchers are deploying artificial intelligence to solve a critical vulnerability in the power grid: the unpredictable aging and decay of large-scale batteries.

The latest research from Xingpeng Li, associate professor at the Cullen College of Engineering, aims to make power grid batteries more efficient as solar and wind power expand.

These sources increasingly depend on battery energy storage systems to balance swings in energy supply, which is stored when energy is high and released when output drops. The problem is batteries degrade over time, reducing their performance and efficiency, which makes it difficult for grid operators to know when to use them.

"The growing use of alternative energy and battery storage in bulk power systems and small-scale microgrid systems, where solar and wind fluctuations make reliable scheduling more challenging," Li said. "A key motivation was that batteries degrade over time that may affect the system performance especially in later years when battery capacity reduced greatly, so we developed a smarter way to include battery aging in energy planning without making the optimization too slow."

How AI Comes into Play

Li and his team addressed this problem by developing a specialized AI-based model that predicts how batteries degrade under real-world conditions.

The model accounts for various factors that affect battery health, which includes temperature, charge rates and overall usage patterns. Traditionally, simple battery-aging assumptions have been used widely.

Li's method offers a more accurate picture of how quickly a battery will wear down; however, while the models can improve predictions, they are often too complex to use in real-time energy planning.

"Charging and discharging at the right times helps store excess renewable energy and release it when demand is high or renewable output is low," Li said. "It also reduces unnecessary battery wear, which can extend battery life and lower system costs. It will also better enhance grid overall efficiency and reliability."

To address the issue, the team helped design a streamlined version that removes less important connections within the neural network to create a simplified system that maintains accuracy while reducing demands.

That allows the system to incorporate battery degradation predictions into energy scheduling, resulting in faster, more informed decisions about when to charge or discharge batteries.

"AI can help predict battery degradation based on how batteries are discharged and charged, allowing operators to make faster and more informed scheduling decisions," Li said. "By using sparse AI neural network, the model keeps much of the accuracy of deep learning while reducing computational burden, making it more practical for day-ahead and real-time operations."

Li said this process could potentially lead to lower prices for electricity.

"For consumers, this could mean a more reliable grid, better use of alternative energy and lower electricity costs over time," he said. "Smarter battery scheduling can also reduce congestion and improve the stability of renewable-heavy power systems."

Battery Improvement Plan

Scientists at UH have long-pursued techniques that could improve battery life, the latest being when engineering Professor Yan Yao exposed structural weaknesses that drive lithium-ion battery failures.

And prior to that, Yao and his team uncovered what caused solid-state batteries to break down and how that process could be slowed. Unlocking that secret was the first step to improving battery life for everything from cell phones and laptops to electric vehicles.

Adding to that potential, Bo Zhao, an award-winning and internationally recognized engineering professor at the Cullen College of Engineering, discovered a technique to control the flow of heat in electronics, which would ultimately help prolong battery endurance.

Part of a Growing AI Research Portfolio

Li's research is part of a robust and expanding body of work at the University of Houston, where AI is being treated as a primary engine for industrial progress. More than 140 faculty researchers are leading over $70 million in AI-related funded research.

This portfolio spans critical sectors including healthcare, resilient infrastructure, cybersecurity and advanced mathematics -- advancing innovations that are essential to both the Houston economy and national competitiveness.

* * *

value.Title: Fervo Energy 's IPO is a milestone for next-gen geothermal

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJX-4963-RYBP-P485-00000-00Fervo Energy's IPO is a milestone for next-gen geothermal2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:59:05ZLexisNexisFervo Energy's IPO is a milestone for next-gen geothermal

The Newstex Authoritative Content shall be construed as author-based content and commentary. Accordingly, no warranties or other guarantees are offered as to the quality of the opinions, commentary or anything else appearing in such Newstex Authoritative Content. Newstex and its re-distributors expressly reserve the right to delete stories at its and their sole discretion.

Dan McCarthy

May 13th, 2026 ( Canary Media - Delivered by Newstex )

Fervo Energy, a startup that has pioneered new ways to produce electricity from the earth's heat, is officially a publicly traded company. It's the first next-generation geothermal firm to go public.

Today's initial public offering netted the Houston-based Fervo about $1.9 billion and valued it at roughly $7.7 billion. The company had reportedly sought a much lower valuation of between $2 billion and $3 billion in January but eventually raised its target amid strong investor interest. Fervo secured nearly $2 billion in financing over the course of its nine years as a private firm.

The debut is a major moment for geothermal energy, which can deliver carbon-free power around the clock but has remained a marginal source of electricity worldwide given its serious geological limitations. Fervomakes geothermal energy viable in far more places by harnessing horizontal drilling techniques borrowed from the oil-and-gas industry, for which its CEO and co-founder, Tim Latimer, previously worked.

Fervo's upsized IPO reflects investor exuberance for any company promising to help meet gargantuan power demand from AI data centers. Fervo has particularly tight ties with Google, which is both an investor in and a customer of the firm. Meta has signed deals with two other advanced geothermal startups in recent years.

"Fervo going public reflects growing confidence in the ability of new geothermal technology to serve soaring electricity demand across the country," John Coequyt, director of U.S. government affairs at clean energy think tank RMI, said in an email.

Fervo joins longtime geothermal leader Ormat on the public market. Ormat, which completed its IPO in 2004, has been building traditional geothermal power plants in the U.S. and beyond for decades, and it recently began expanding its focus to include ' enhanced geothermal systems' like Fervo's. Ormat saw its stock price climb steadily for years and then nearly double over the last year and change.

Fervo's IPO comes months ahead of another expected milestone for the startup: the commissioning of its first-of-a-kind power plant in Utah. The development, dubbed Cape Station, broke ground in 2023 and is on track to start sending electricity to the grid in late 2026. A total of 500 megawatts are under construction at the site, but Fervo has the permits in place to quadruple that amount.

Fervo also plans to bring a 115-MW development in Nevada online by 2030, as part of its power purchase agreement with Google and utility NV Energy.

The money Fervo has raised with its IPO should help the company acquire new land, defray costs related to developing projects, and fund general operations. In its IPO filing, Fervo identified a total of 3.65 gigawatts of power plant capacity that is under construction, ready to be built, or in advanced stages of development. The U.S. currently has roughly 4 GW of installed geothermal capacity.

Fervo's success will depend on its ability to drive down the cost of the power it produces.

The Cape Station project is set to deliver power at $7,000 per kilowatt, a price that is competitive with traditional and next-generation nuclear power but far higher than that of natural gas or renewables. The company aims to slash that rate to $3,000 per kW.

Fervo has shown some ability to cut costs to date. Between 2022 and 2025, Fervo says it has reduced drilling times by about 75% and slashed per-foot drilling costs by about 70%, marking a significant achievement for the nascent industry. Those trends will need to hold up as the company completes larger-scale installations in the years to come.

Fervo expects to run a loss for 'several years,' per its IPO document, as it spends more aggressively to build out its power plants. Its net loss was roughly $57.8 million last year, up from $41.1 million the year prior.

Revenue was a scant $138,000 last year - but Fervo's IPO document says there is a lot more waiting in the wings. To date, it has signed 658 megawatts' worth of binding power purchase agreements with major utility Southern California Edison, community choice aggregators, and firms like Google and Shell. That adds up to 'approximately $7.2 billion in potential revenue backlog,' per the filing.

It also has an agreement in place with Google, whereby Fervo will give the tech giant the right of first refusal to purchase 3 GW of electricity from certain new projects, though Google itself is under no obligation to say yes. Either party can terminate the deal if no binding commitments have been made by March 2028.

Geothermal energy enjoys more bipartisan support in the U.S. than any other renewable energy source.

While President Donald Trump's One Big Beautiful Bill Act sunset federal tax credits for solar and wind this July, those for geothermal were left intact. The fracking firm founded and formerly led by Energy Secretary Chris Wright invested in Fervo in 2022. Not one but two bipartisan pro-geothermal bills are under consideration in Congress right now.

And although the Trump administration continues to obstruct wind and solar projects on federal lands, next month the Interior Department is slated to auction off an additional 197,000 acres of land in New Mexico for geothermal energy development.

Maria Gallucci contributed reporting to this piece.

Link to the original story.

InvestorBrandNetwork (IBN)

May 8th, 2026 ( InvestorBrandNetwork - Delivered by Newstex )

Norway recorded a 98.6% EV shareof new passenger car registrations in April, breaking the prior record of 98.4% set just one month earlier. Of April's 11,103 registrations, 10,952 were fully electric, whilejust 87 were diesel and 31 petrol, according to the OFV, Norway's Road Federation.

If a similar trend of EV uptake plays out in other markets over the coming years, we are likely to see a lot higher sales numbers from entities like Massimo Group (NASDAQ: MAMO)

value.Title: AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. - RECENT DEVELOPMENTS WITH KIEWIT CORPORATION AND OTHERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK4-7343-RSHD-P308-00000-00AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. - RECENT DEVELOPMENTS WITH KIEWIT CORPORATION AND OTHERS2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:13:28ZLexisNexisSTOCKAUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. - RECENT DEVELOPMENTS WITH KIEWIT CORPORATION AND OTHERS

DASHBOARD: AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC.

PermID4296692948Websitehttp://www.austin-ind.comIndustryConstructionAddress3535 Travis Street Suite 300 Dallas, TX 75204

ACTIVITIES:

Austin Industries, Inc., founded in 1918, is a privately-owned construction firm headquartered in Dallas, Texas. Employing over 7,000 people, it operates through subsidiaries like Austin Commercial and Austin Bridge & Road, serving commercial, infrastructure, and industrial sectors. With annual revenues exceeding $2 billion, Austin delivers projects like airports, highways, and corporate campuses. In 2024, it expanded its portfolio with sustainable infrastructure projects, including renewable energy facilities. Its employee-owned culture fosters accountability, safety, and collaboration, with robust training programs. Competing with Balfour Beatty, Austin stands out for its regional expertise and commitment to quality. Recent initiatives include adopting advanced construction technologies like BIM and focusing on green building certifications, aligning with industry sustainability trends.

+ AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ AUSTIN NAMED NO. 1 SCIENCE + TECHNOLOGY CONSTRUCTION FIRM

DALLAS - Austin is honored to once again be named the No. 1 Science + Technology Facility Construction Firm in the U.S. by World of Construction.

‍

From semiconductor fabs to digital infrastructure, our teams pour their skill, care, and dedication into creating the specialized spaces that help fuel tomorrow's breakthroughs. It's thanks to our employee‑owners, partners, and clients that this work is possible, and we're grateful for the trust they place in us every day.

‍

Firms recognized by World of Construction are those that are leading the way in delivering some of the most advanced science and technology facilities in the world. Austin is grateful for the recognition and proud to be leading our industry.

Source: Company Website

+ AUSTIN EARNS MULTIPLE HONORS AT ASA HOUSTON EVENT

HOUSTON - Austin Commercial was honored to be finalists for multiple Excellence in Construction Awards from American Subcontractors Association-Houston Chapter. Austin's Houston office was awarded the GC Safety Award and the Project of the Year Over $50M for the UH Memorial Hermann Football Operations Center.

‍

The company extends its thanks to our trade partners and employee-owners for all that you do, and for making these honors possible.

Source: Company Website

+ AUSTIN RANKED #27 ON BD+C'S TOP 100 HOSPITAL FACILITY CONSTRUCTION FIRMS

DALLAS - Austin has been named No. 27 on Building Design + Construction's (BD+C) 2025 Top 100 Hospital Facility Construction Firms list, reaffirming the company's long-standing position as a trusted builder in the healthcare market, and highlighting continued growth in this sector.

Austin has appeared on BD+C's healthcare rankings for years, but recent performance reflects a notable expansion in both volume and opportunity. That momentum is already visible in the work underway. In 2025, Austin began preconstruction on several major healthcare projects, including JPS Health Network Hospital Addition, The University of Texas at Austin & The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center Medical Complex Project (MCP), and The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center Patient Care Building 1 (1PC).

This ranking is a testament to the expertise and dedication of Austin's employee-owners and highlights the importance of continued investment in people and resources as healthcare remains a key growth sector for the company.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 Press Releases]

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION PROTECTING WHAT LIES BELOW

Near the base of Nch'kay', or Mount Garibaldi, the Squamish community, in western Canada, has lived for decades with the threat of large debris flows. These fast-moving surges of mud, water, trees and boulders can cascade down the river with little warning. The Cheekye River, whose name comes from the Skwxwu7mesh (Squamish Nation language) word for "muddy waters," has a long history of these events.

Because of the historical risk, development on the alluvial fan - the broad deposit of sediment formed where the river spreads out at the base of the mountain - had been restricted for years. Despite many existing homes and businesses on the fan being in harm's way, little had been done to reduce the risk. Sqomish Sea to Sky Developments, a partnership between the Squamish Nation and local land development company Matthews West, owns just over 500 acres of this land.

Source: Company Website

+ KIEWIT'S 85 YEARS IN CANADA

In 2026, Kiewit proudly marks its 85th year of building in Canada. The company's Canadian story began in 1941 in the North, supporting construction of the Norman Wells Oil Refinery and Pipeline. In 1949, Kiewit opened its first Canadian office in Vancouver, establishing a permanent presence in the country. From those early roots, the company expanded across regions and markets, building the relationships, workforce and operational capacity required to serve clients coast to coast to coast. For the past 85 years, Kiewit has delivered complex projects across transportation, energy, marine, water, industrial and emerging sectors that support a changing economy.

"Diversifying our markets has made us a stronger, more resilient business," said Ryan Tones, president of Peter Kiewit Sons ULC. "Our teams have built hydroelectric facilities, ports and marine infrastructure, highways and transit systems, pipelines and power facilities, and essential water and wastewater systems that protect communities and natural resources. Our investment in our Marine Yard on the West Coast and our continued growth in water, wastewater and mining, reflect our commitment to serving Canada's evolving infrastructure needs and creating long-term opportunities for our people and partners."

Today, Kiewit employs more than 4,000 people across Canada, including a strong and growing craft workforce. Positioned across the country, these teams are building on decades of experience while preparing for Canada's next chapter.

The following timeline highlights select milestones and projects from the past 85 years and illustrates Kiewit's enduring role in advancing Canada's energy, infrastructure and economic development.

Source: Company Website

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION TIME CAPSULE: RECORD-BREAKING BRIDGES

From bustling city centers to remote wilderness areas, bridges stand as testaments to human ingenuity and the relentless pursuit of connection. Over the years, Kiewit has contributed to several record-breaking bridges unlike any before their time. This collection of Kieways articles revisits those milestone projects.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. [4 Press Releases]

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. MORE THAN 160 YEARS OF BUILDING FORWARD

For more than 160 years, McCarthy has been shaped by people who came before, building a legacy grounded in craftsmanship, integrity and long-term vision. This second edition of the company's history book captures the evolution from its earliest roots to a modern, employee-owned organization committed to innovation, partnership and great experiences for everyone. Along the way, it traces the milestones, bold decisions and defining projects that helped establish McCarthy as one of the industry's most respected builders. It is both a reflection on where McCarthy has been and a celebration of the ownership mindset shaping where it goes next.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. BAYTOWN AREA WATER AUTHORITY AND BAYTOWN WATERWORKS PARTNERS BREAK GROUND ON EAST SURFACE WATER TREATMENT PLANT PHASE II

Baytown Area Water Authority and Baytown Waterworks Partners, a joint venture between McCarthy Building Companies, Inc. and Carollo Engineers, are pleased to announce they have broken ground on Phase II of the Baytown Area Water Authority East Surface Water Treatment Plant, a progressive design-build expansion that will increase the facility's treatment capacity from 6 million gallons per day (MGD) to 12 MGD. This milestone marks a major investment in long-term, reliable surface water supplies for the Baytown area.

Through a progressive design-build delivery, Baytown Waterworks Partners has collaborated closely with Baytown Area Water Authority, in conjunction with BGE Engineering who serves as the Owner's Advisor, from early design and will continue to collaborate through construction to advance technical solutions, manage risk, and efficiently deliver the expanded facilities. The plant expansion is expected to be fully functional at target capacity by summer 2028.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. SERVICE AFTER SERVICE: VETERANS BUILDING HOUSTON'S FUTURE WITH MCCARTHY

When sailors and Marines arrived in Houston for its first-ever hosted Fleet Week, most people looked up - to the ships, the helicopters and the flyovers. At McCarthy, a lot of our partners look out - to the wharves, docks and water infrastructure they now help build along the same channels those vessels call home.

For veterans like Andy Garcia, Corey Everitte and John Walker, Fleet Week isn't just a celebration of military service. It's a reminder that their mission hasn't ended with military retirement; it has just evolved to helping build and protect the infrastructure that keeps a maritime city moving.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. TAKING PRIDE IN SAFETY: A CONVERSATION WITH BILLY NAYLOR

Having the privilege of leading national safety for McCarthy Building Companies, Inc. is one of the greatest honors of my life. It's something I'm extraordinarily proud of and something I never thought I'd accomplish. I want every employee to know there is not a day I take it for granted, not a day I don't take it seriously and not a day I'm not pulling hard for everyone to return home safely. Q: Serious Injury and Fatality (SIF) prevention is one of McCarthy's highest safety priorities for 2026. What makes SIF events different from other incidents, and what specific steps is McCarthy's national safety team taking this year to get in front of them before they happen?

A: SIF requires a mindset shift. That's the most important thing to understand about it.

For years, the construction industry was wired to think about safety through the lens of recordability. Our recordable rate is something we're mandated to submit on every proposal, every owner deliverable. And companies - including ours - have gotten very good at driving that number down. When I joined McCarthy in 2006, our rates were much higher. Reducing recordable incidents became the driver of our behaviors and the primary way we measured ourselves.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: Cromarty Green Hydrogen Project's future secured after being acquired by Protium in £100m investment; the project is located not far from the Cromarty Firth near the Beinn Tharsuinn Windfarm in Easter Ross
value.Overview: Ross-shire Journal, MERGERS AND ACQUISITIONS NEWS & ENVIRONMENT NEWS, 611words

urn:contentItem:6JK4-Y5W3-RXF7-C09M-00000-00Cromarty Green Hydrogen Project's future secured after being acquired by Protium in £100m investment; the project is located not far from the Cromarty Firth near the Beinn Tharsuinn Windfarm in Easter Ross2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:13:28ZLexisNexisMERGERS AND ACQUISITIONS NEWS & ENVIRONMENT NEWSCromarty Green Hydrogen Project's future secured after being acquired by Protium in £100m investment; the project is located not far from the Cromarty Firth near the Beinn Tharsuinn Windfarm in Easter Rossphilip.murray@hnmedia.co.uk (Philip Murray)

A major green energy scheme in the Highlands has "secured its future" after Protium acquired the from Storegga as part of a £100m investment.

The 15MW project, located east of the Beinn Tharsuinn Windfarm not far from the B9176 Struie road, is aimed at decarbonising Highland industries, , while supporting local jobs and introducing a new community benefit fund tied to production capacity.

The green hydrogen project had been a , but questions were raised about its future last autumn when Scottish Power paused its UK green hydrogen plans.

But the take-over of the project from Storegga - as well as Storegga's wider Scottish development portfolio - has secured its immediate future, with Protium saying the move will help ensure the development remains rooted in Scotland.

"We are proud to become the custodians of the Cromarty project," said Christopher Jackson, CEO of Protium. "Our focus is energy certainty. By localising production, we ensure that the Highlands' world-class industry and transport sectors aren't just surviving the Net Zero transition, they are leading it".

Protium already operates the UK's first and largest commercial green hydrogen plant, Pioneer 1, in Wales.

Building on that momentum, Protium's Pioneer 2 flagship facility (2.5MW) has recently achieved "first gas" and is now in its commissioning phase. Once fully operational in June, Pioneer 2 will scale production to one tonne of low-carbon hydrogen per day. Protium project this will abate approximately 3600 tonnes of CO2 annually - the environmental equivalent of removing 2,200 cars from UK roads every year.

Beyond Wales and Scotland, Protium is also progressing projects in South Tees.

Those behind the Cromarty project claim it will produce approximately 2000 tonnes per annum.

With offices in London, Port Talbot and Edinburgh, the green hydrogen company says it is committed to creating high-skilled jobs and apprenticeships within the Highland Council area and beyond.

Those behind the Cromarty Hydrogen Project , and more to follow as it develops further.

Protium views itself as a long-term partner in the Highland economy and said it is committed to an open dialogue with local communities, which it claims will ensure insights and feedback inform how the 15MW electrolyser plans are designed and delivered.

The Cromarty project was a cornerstone of the UK government's Hydrogen Allocation Round 1 (HAR1), administered by the Department for Energy Security and Net Zero.

Mr Jackson says Protium's Wales developments, acquisition of the Cromarty project and plans for Teeside will boost UK energy security at a crucial time.

"This acquisition materially strengthens our national platform and bolsters UK energy security by anchoring production within our own borders," he said.

"Protium is building permanent, industrial-scale green hydrogen infrastructure that decarbonises British industry today. With operational assets in Wales, presence in Scotland, and projects advancing in South Tees, we are scaling toward a 1GW UK portfolio to establish a resilient, self-sufficient, and competitive domestic hydrogen economy."

Welcoming the acquisition of the Cromarty project, Tim Stedman, CEO of Storegga, added: "Over recent years, Storegga has established a strong portfolio of green hydrogen opportunities in Scotland.

"Our priority has been to ensure these projects move forward under an established developer, with the platform and capability to take them into delivery.

"Protium's track record and growing UK presence position them well to advance these projects, and we look forward to seeing their full potential realised to support a more sustainable industrial future in Scotland."

An artist's impression of the Cromarty project's design, subject to detailed development, technical assessments, and planning approvals.

value.Title: IEEFA: Court Ruling on Texas Anti-ESG Law May Save State From Self-inflicted Economic Wound

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK7-DKF3-RX82-91X4-00000-00IEEFA: Court Ruling on Texas Anti-ESG Law May Save State From Self-inflicted Economic Wound2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:13:28ZLexisNexisIEEFA: Court Ruling on Texas Anti-ESG Law May Save State From Self-inflicted Economic WoundTargeted News ServiceWASHINGTON

(TNSLrpt) -- The Institute for Energy Economics and Financial Analysis issued the following briefing note on March 30, 2026 by Dan Cohn and Suzanne Mattei entitled "Court ruling on Texas anti-ESG law may save state from self-inflicted economic wound."

Here are excerpts:

* * *

Key Findings

A federal court ruling striking down a Texas "anti-ESG" law may have far-reaching effects.

The law was designed to prevent public pension managers and government contractors from boycotting fossil fuel companies.

The state's description of the oil and gas industry as the "economic lifeblood" of Texas is based on outdated and incorrect information.

Two expert studies found the statute increased the cost of municipal bond issuance by Texas by hundreds of millions of dollars.

Introduction

A federal court ruling striking down a Texas "anti-ESG" law essentially designed to prevent public pension managers from divesting from fossil fuel assets may save the state from a self-inflicted economic wound.

Although the decision affects only the first of a wave of proposals aimed at promoting fossil fuel investments while suppressing support for renewable energy, it casts significant doubt on the legality of similar state efforts. The implications may have far-reaching effects for investors, policymakers, and fiduciaries across the country.

The Texas attorney general and state comptroller plan to appeal the ruling. However, the law, which was designed to protect the oil and gas industry, is based on a flawed assumption. A policy designed to restrict investments and favor an obsolete economic development model is not likely to achieve the desired result.

If companies identify sustainable non-fossil fuel investments that present good financial opportunities, they should be able to take lawful business actions to pursue their interests without government interference.

This premise is consistent with the U.S. Constitution and longstanding practices of enterprise in America's economic system. Corporate boards and senior managers are expected to consider issues that may affect a company's bottom line. Awareness of the potential impacts of environmental, social, and governance (ESG) factors, including climate change, is part of responsible corporate governance.

* * *

View full briefing note at: https://ieefa.org/resources/court-ruling-texas-anti-esg-law-may-save-state-self-inflicted-economic-wound

value.Title: M . A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - COMPREHENSIVE INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS UPDATES

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK7-WX83-S1DX-409X-00000-00M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - COMPREHENSIVE INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS UPDATES2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:13:28ZLexisNexisSTOCKM. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - COMPREHENSIVE INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS UPDATES

DASHBOARD: M A MORTENSON

PermID5001069053Websitehttp://www.mortenson.comIndustryConstruction

ACTIVITIES:

M. A. Mortenson Companies Inc., founded in 1954 by M.A. Mortenson Sr., is a top-20 U.S. builder headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota. With 5,000 employees across 12 offices, it delivers construction, development, and energy services, generating $5.7 billion in 2023 revenue. Mortenson specializes in healthcare, renewable energy, and data centers, with projects like Allianz Field. Its integrated services emphasize sustainability and innovation, using advanced technologies like digital modeling. Competing with Gilbane, Mortensons commitment to safety and diversity, along with its North American operations, ensures high-performing project delivery.

+ M A MORTENSON [1 News That Matters]

+ U.S. ARMY CORPS FUNDS $610M FACILITY TO PRODUCE STERILE SCREWWORMS; MORTENSON TO LEAD CONSTRUCTION

M A Mortenson Company has won a $610 million contract from the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers to construct the Domestic New World Screwworm Sterile Insect Production Facility at Moore Airbase in Edinburg, Texas. The bid supports ongoing efforts to prevent the New World screwworm from spreading into the United States from Mexico, where authorities report rising activity near the Texas border. As of March 4, 2026, there were 989 active cases nationwide, including 19 in Tamaulipas, a rise from 11 weeks prior, underscoring the need for robust control measures such as the deployment of sterilized flies along the border. Mortenson’s facility will play a pivotal role in ensuring a reliable supply of sterile insects, reinforcing the USDA’s efforts to protect livestock and sustain farm income in border states.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ANNOUNCES 9,100-SF INNSBROOK EXPANSION UNDER COUNTY INCENTIVES

DPR Construction plans to expand its Innsbrook office by 9,100 square feet, backed by incentives from Henrico County. The date of this announcement is 29th April 2026.The expansion is designed to accommodate a growing workforce and enhance operations in the region.Officials say the project reflects confidence in the local market and will create additional job opportunities while reinforcing the firm’s sustainability commitments.The design will feature modern elements and sustainable practices, aligning with DPR’s mission to lead in sustainable construction.Completion is anticipated within the next year.

+ DPR TAPS JASON PAGE TO LEAD NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP AND EXPAND INTERIOR BUILDOUTS

The SSG handles a range of work from parking garage stair installations to interior buildouts, and under Page's leadership the group aims to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs while Page mentors teams and promotes best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION ACHIEVES GREAT PLACE TO WORK CERTIFICATION WITH 97% POSITIVE EMPLOYEE FEEDBACK

DPR Construction has earned Great Place To Work Certification after 97 percent of its employees affirmed it is a great place to work, a rate about 40 points above the national average. The certification, awarded on 31st March 2026, highlights a culture built on pride, belonging and opportunity.According to CEO George Pfeffer, the certification reflects the real sentiments of the people who carry the company’s culture. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said, underscoring an emphasis on trust, inclusion, and growth.DPR’s approach centers on empowering employees to make decisions and share information openly across projects. This openness supports initiative, whether in innovating on complex tasks, taking on new roles, or offering ideas outside formal job descriptions, with learning and development programs backing career progression.The recognition complements DPR’s broader reputation as an employer of choice and its ongoing achievements, including industry accolades and expansion across markets. DPR, founded in 1990, has grown into a multi billion dollar contractor employing over 11,000 professionals worldwide, with innovations such as virtual design and construction and self performing craft.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION ROLLS OUT COMPLIMENTARY AI TOOL TO STRENGTHEN ON-SITE SAFETY CULTURE

Turner Construction has introduced SafeT Coach, a zero-cost AI safety assistant that works on mobile to support real-time safety conversations on job sites. The AI leverages Turner’s Environmental Health & Safety standards rather than general internet content and was announced on 9th May 2026. Designed to cultivate a coaching mindset among supervisors and craft professionals, SafeT Coach presents outputs as potential hazards to help users apply professional judgment. The tool evolved with input from more than 80 stakeholders through design exercises and field pilots, and has logged over 25,000 interactions across Turner staff, trade partners, and field teams. In one example, a supervisor evaluated whether a vertical shaft was permit-required confined space by using a decision flow, permit checklist, and policy citations provided by the tool, facilitating a safer discussion with a partner's safety manager. The company says the free tool could raise safety standards industry-wide if widely adopted.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION SECURES LEAD ROLE ON WESTCOURT DOWNTOWN ORLANDO $500M PROJECT

Turner Construction Company has been selected as general contractor for Westcourt, a $500 million mixed-use development in downtown Orlando, announced on 5th May 2026. The project has faced delays but gains renewed momentum with Turner at the helm.The development spans 8.5 acres and will provide 900,000 to 1,000,000 square feet of space, including a 265-key Kimpton hotel, 269 high-rise residential units, and up to 310,000 square feet of Class A office space.Other elements include more than 125,000 square feet of dining and entertainment, a 3,500-capacity venue for live events, and 1,140 on-site parking spaces. Peak construction will involve around 800 on-site workers, with about 1,600 direct construction jobs over the project’s life. Turner plans to partner with Central Florida trade firms to maximize local benefits.Key milestones include entitlements secured in April 2024, the Kimpton partnership announced later that year, and a $2.5 million commitment from Orlando’s Community Redevelopment Agency, potentially reimbursing up to $40 million through future property tax growth. Groundbreaking is targeted for Q2 2026, with a late-2028 opening anticipated.

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION JOINS WEWORK INDIA FOR LARGE-SCALE MUMBAI WORKSPACE

Turner Construction is expanding its India footprint with a 46,000-square-foot WeWork India managed workspace in Airoli, Mumbai, opened on the 7th of May, 2026, and serving more than 650 professionals.The office was designed exclusively for Turner, aligning with global standards while leveraging WeWork India’s design capabilities. Jairam Panch, Chief Operating Officer of Turner Construction, says the space is more than a location, fostering collaboration, creativity, and excellence.Turner first collaborated with WeWork India in 2022 with a 16-seat private office, and the Airoli site was chosen for its connectivity, talent access, and growing business ecosystem. The workspace integrates the company s precision-focused approach with WeWork India s design thinking and features collaborative spaces and modern amenities.The shift toward managed offices reflects a broader trend in enterprise workspaces. Santosh Martin, Chief Revenue Officer of WeWork India, notes that companies increasingly view work environments as drivers of performance and culture. Turner’s move from a small office to a larger, customized space exemplifies a flexible model that supports growth while preserving corporate identity.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ JE DUNN DEBUTS FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ELEVATE PROJECT EFFICIENCY

JE Dunn Construction has unveiled Form Off-Site Solutions, a new off-site manufacturing initiative focused on Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex components. The launch occurred 6th April 2026, signaling the company’s intent to advance its Kansas City legacy into a wider market.Led by Nick Effenheim, the team merges skilled craftspeople with automation to produce metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies.The service model spans estimating, design, manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, with the goal of improving installation efficiency, safety, and overall quality while helping reduce costs and shorten timelines.Effenheim notes, "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and turn ideas into a manufactured reality is what sets Form apart." The Kansas City and Nashville facilities position Form for national work.

+ NORTHLAND WORKFORCE CENTER ADVANCES WITH TOPPING-OUT CEREMONY

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — JE Dunn Construction marked a topping-out milestone for the Northland Workforce Development Center on 19th March 2026, with the final steel beam installed on the 145,000-square-foot, two-story facility.A beam-signing ceremony drew attendees who left signatures and messages on a beam bearing project and partner logos, underscoring collaboration among the Platte County R-3 School District, partner districts, stakeholders, and the community. Project Executive Brad Hokanson commented, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The center is designed to support programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services, expanding capacity for Northland Career Center and partners to serve high school students and adult learners seeking credentials. Planned spaces include flexible labs, classrooms, hands-on training areas, and technology-rich environments that mirror real-world workplaces.The project, located in the Northland region of Kansas City, is on schedule with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening anticipated after the milestone. JE Dunn is serving as construction manager in partnership with the district and Clark & Enersen, prioritizing safety, coordination, and ongoing communication with stakeholders to minimize disruption to nearby facilities.

+ CHELETTE RETURNS TO JE DUNN TO LEAD HEALTHCARE CONSTRUCTION GROWTH IN THE SOUTHEAST

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025. During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region. In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction. Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively. As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GILBANE [3 News That Matters]

+ GILBANE, OSHA SIGN SAFETY ALLIANCE AT NATIONAL MALL EVENT DURING CONSTRUCTION SAFETY WEEK

Nearly 1,000 industry professionals gathered on the National Mall as Gilbane Building, chair of Construction Safety Week 2026, led the signing of a new alliance with OSHA on May 12, 2026, alongside the 13th National Safety Stand-Down to Prevent Falls in Construction.The alliance seeks to strengthen collaboration between industry and government to prevent injuries and fatalities and to advance Total Worker Health across the project lifecycle. It emphasizes safety education, training resources, and consistent safety frameworks, with a focus on wellbeing and broad stakeholder engagement from planning through operations.Gilbane’s Adam Jelen, president and CEO and week chair, described the alliance as a unifying step for the industry. The event highlighted the week’s theme, All in Together, anchored by Recognize, Respond, Respect, which calls for increased vigilance in high-hazard settings. A new technical bulletin series, developed with safety experts and craft professionals, aims to help manage high-risk hazards proactively and align with the framework. The Stand-Down remains a staple nationwide effort to reinforce fall prevention training and hazard awareness.

+ GILBANE DEVELOPMENT ELEVATES ANDREAS VLAHAKIS TO HEAD OF CAPITAL STRATEGY

As of 28th April 2026, Gilbane Development named Andreas Vlahakis as head of capital markets, based in the firm's New York City office.He will refine and execute the funding strategy, reporting to president and CEO James Patchett, and will help guide capital strategy and investment decisions while building relationships with funding and equity partners.Vlahakis previously served as managing director for capital markets at Thor Equities, where he led equity-raising efforts for a range of projects and partnerships. His prior role as senior vice president and chief of staff at Thor Equities highlights his breadth of financial leadership.Gilbane says the appointment strengthens its capital program and supports its growth plans in core sectors.

+ GILBANE-WKU PPP AIMS TO MODERNIZE HOUSING, EXPAND STUDENT RESOURCES

WKU announced the approval of a public-private partnership with Gilbane to develop a new residence hall replacing Keen and Poland Halls, with the Board of Regents' approval on 11th December 2025. The Kentucky Legislature's approval on 6th April 2026 clears the way for construction. SGA Senator Barker stated the partnership is fully authorized and that WKU is now ready to begin building. The SGA also advanced measures to benefit students, including Resolution 7-26-S for a crosswalk near housing and collaboration with the Kentucky DOT, and Bill 17-26-S providing $50 reimbursements for textbooks, along with provisions for Scantrons and lab goggles.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ CARLY REINER CACERES TAKES HELM OF HR FOR NATIONAL CAMPUS RECRUITING AT CLARK CONSTRUCTION

Carly Reiner Caceres has assumed the role of Director of Human Resources – National Campus Recruiting at Clark Construction Group, effective Apr. 29, 2026, tasked with coordinating nationwide campus initiatives to expand early-career talent pipelines for the company.Her career blends leadership in People Operations at Proper, where she guided HR strategy in a hybrid environment, with roles at MILLER as Director of Internal Operations and nearly a decade at Accenture as Talent Strategy Manager and Campus Recruiting Lead.Caceres’ experience spans full-cycle recruiting, stakeholder engagement, and employer branding, capabilities Clark Construction aims to leverage to attract top talent from universities across the United States. She has also led campus recruiting efforts at Grant Thornton LLP, strengthening internship programs and student engagement.Clark Construction, founded in 1906, emphasizes community impact and project excellence. Caceres’ background in communications and external relations from early roles at Charleston Magazine and the Hirshhorn Museum is expected to support her work in nurturing the next generation of construction professionals.

+ CLARK CONTRACTS TO DELIVER REPAIRS, MAINTENANCE AND REFURBISHMENT UNDER RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL FRAMEWORK

Clark Contracts has been named to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades framework to improve repair, maintenance and refurbishment services for domestic properties in the area. The appointment was announced on 11th February 2026 and runs through 2027.The framework covers a broad range of multi-trade works, enabling the council to better manage both responsive and planned maintenance across its housing stock and to deliver more timely service to residents.Clark Contracts is already active in the public sector, with commitments including Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework, and involvement with Crown Commercial Service and Scottish Procurement Alliance projects.Gordon Cunningham, managing director, noted the company’s track record of more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over three years and said the appointment strengthens the services available to Renfrewshire residents.

+ THE VILLAGES SET FOR ₱4-B MIXED-USE PROJECT AS CDC ENDORSES 30-MONTH BUILD

Clark Development Corp. has approved an amendment to PKR Corp.'s lease for a ₱4-billion mixed-use hub at The Villages, a 67-hectare master-planned sports and lifestyle destination in Clark Freeport. The amendment grants PKR a 30-month timeframe to construct the project and introduces performance safeguards to help ensure on-schedule delivery. CDC president and CEO Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera described the move as reinforcing investor confidence and the community’s integration within The Villages, while PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung expressed gratitude for the partnership and pledged timely completion. PKR operates under Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean builder with recognized strengths in safety, technology, and workplace practices. The development aligns with CDC’s strategy to accelerate investments, foster well-planned communities, and strengthen Clark as a business-friendly destination that attracts both local and international capital.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. - UPDATES FROM KIEWIT CORPORATION AND PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJW-8BN3-S333-S0F6-00000-00AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. - UPDATES FROM KIEWIT CORPORATION AND PEERS2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:13:28ZLexisNexisSTOCKAUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. - UPDATES FROM KIEWIT CORPORATION AND PEERS

DASHBOARD: AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC.

PermID4296692948Websitehttp://www.austin-ind.comIndustryConstructionAddress3535 Travis Street Suite 300 Dallas, TX 75204

ACTIVITIES:

Austin Industries, Inc., founded in 1918, is a privately-owned construction firm headquartered in Dallas, Texas. Employing over 7,000 people, it operates through subsidiaries like Austin Commercial and Austin Bridge & Road, serving commercial, infrastructure, and industrial sectors. With annual revenues exceeding $2 billion, Austin delivers projects like airports, highways, and corporate campuses. In 2024, it expanded its portfolio with sustainable infrastructure projects, including renewable energy facilities. Its employee-owned culture fosters accountability, safety, and collaboration, with robust training programs. Competing with Balfour Beatty, Austin stands out for its regional expertise and commitment to quality. Recent initiatives include adopting advanced construction technologies like BIM and focusing on green building certifications, aligning with industry sustainability trends.

+ AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ AUSTIN NAMED NO. 1 SCIENCE + TECHNOLOGY CONSTRUCTION FIRM

DALLAS - Austin is honored to once again be named the No. 1 Science + Technology Facility Construction Firm in the U.S. by World of Construction.

‍

From semiconductor fabs to digital infrastructure, our teams pour their skill, care, and dedication into creating the specialized spaces that help fuel tomorrow's breakthroughs. It's thanks to our employee‑owners, partners, and clients that this work is possible, and we're grateful for the trust they place in us every day.

‍

Firms recognized by World of Construction are those that are leading the way in delivering some of the most advanced science and technology facilities in the world. Austin is grateful for the recognition and proud to be leading our industry.

Source: Company Website

+ AUSTIN EARNS MULTIPLE HONORS AT ASA HOUSTON EVENT

HOUSTON - Austin Commercial was honored to be finalists for multiple Excellence in Construction Awards from American Subcontractors Association-Houston Chapter. Austin's Houston office was awarded the GC Safety Award and the Project of the Year Over $50M for the UH Memorial Hermann Football Operations Center.

‍

The company extends its thanks to our trade partners and employee-owners for all that you do, and for making these honors possible.

Source: Company Website

+ AUSTIN RANKED #27 ON BD+C'S TOP 100 HOSPITAL FACILITY CONSTRUCTION FIRMS

DALLAS - Austin has been named No. 27 on Building Design + Construction's (BD+C) 2025 Top 100 Hospital Facility Construction Firms list, reaffirming the company's long-standing position as a trusted builder in the healthcare market, and highlighting continued growth in this sector.

Austin has appeared on BD+C's healthcare rankings for years, but recent performance reflects a notable expansion in both volume and opportunity. That momentum is already visible in the work underway. In 2025, Austin began preconstruction on several major healthcare projects, including JPS Health Network Hospital Addition, The University of Texas at Austin & The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center Medical Complex Project (MCP), and The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center Patient Care Building 1 (1PC).

This ranking is a testament to the expertise and dedication of Austin's employee-owners and highlights the importance of continued investment in people and resources as healthcare remains a key growth sector for the company.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 Press Releases]

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION PROTECTING WHAT LIES BELOW

Near the base of Nch'kay', or Mount Garibaldi, the Squamish community, in western Canada, has lived for decades with the threat of large debris flows. These fast-moving surges of mud, water, trees and boulders can cascade down the river with little warning. The Cheekye River, whose name comes from the Skwxwu7mesh (Squamish Nation language) word for "muddy waters," has a long history of these events.

Because of the historical risk, development on the alluvial fan - the broad deposit of sediment formed where the river spreads out at the base of the mountain - had been restricted for years. Despite many existing homes and businesses on the fan being in harm's way, little had been done to reduce the risk. Sqomish Sea to Sky Developments, a partnership between the Squamish Nation and local land development company Matthews West, owns just over 500 acres of this land.

Source: Company Website

+ KIEWIT'S 85 YEARS IN CANADA

In 2026, Kiewit proudly marks its 85th year of building in Canada. The company's Canadian story began in 1941 in the North, supporting construction of the Norman Wells Oil Refinery and Pipeline. In 1949, Kiewit opened its first Canadian office in Vancouver, establishing a permanent presence in the country. From those early roots, the company expanded across regions and markets, building the relationships, workforce and operational capacity required to serve clients coast to coast to coast. For the past 85 years, Kiewit has delivered complex projects across transportation, energy, marine, water, industrial and emerging sectors that support a changing economy.

"Diversifying our markets has made us a stronger, more resilient business," said Ryan Tones, president of Peter Kiewit Sons ULC. "Our teams have built hydroelectric facilities, ports and marine infrastructure, highways and transit systems, pipelines and power facilities, and essential water and wastewater systems that protect communities and natural resources. Our investment in our Marine Yard on the West Coast and our continued growth in water, wastewater and mining, reflect our commitment to serving Canada's evolving infrastructure needs and creating long-term opportunities for our people and partners."

Today, Kiewit employs more than 4,000 people across Canada, including a strong and growing craft workforce. Positioned across the country, these teams are building on decades of experience while preparing for Canada's next chapter.

The following timeline highlights select milestones and projects from the past 85 years and illustrates Kiewit's enduring role in advancing Canada's energy, infrastructure and economic development.

Source: Company Website

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION TIME CAPSULE: RECORD-BREAKING BRIDGES

From bustling city centers to remote wilderness areas, bridges stand as testaments to human ingenuity and the relentless pursuit of connection. Over the years, Kiewit has contributed to several record-breaking bridges unlike any before their time. This collection of Kieways articles revisits those milestone projects.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. [4 Press Releases]

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. MORE THAN 160 YEARS OF BUILDING FORWARD

For more than 160 years, McCarthy has been shaped by people who came before, building a legacy grounded in craftsmanship, integrity and long-term vision. This second edition of the company's history book captures the evolution from its earliest roots to a modern, employee-owned organization committed to innovation, partnership and great experiences for everyone. Along the way, it traces the milestones, bold decisions and defining projects that helped establish McCarthy as one of the industry's most respected builders. It is both a reflection on where McCarthy has been and a celebration of the ownership mindset shaping where it goes next.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. BAYTOWN AREA WATER AUTHORITY AND BAYTOWN WATERWORKS PARTNERS BREAK GROUND ON EAST SURFACE WATER TREATMENT PLANT PHASE II

Baytown Area Water Authority and Baytown Waterworks Partners, a joint venture between McCarthy Building Companies, Inc. and Carollo Engineers, are pleased to announce they have broken ground on Phase II of the Baytown Area Water Authority East Surface Water Treatment Plant, a progressive design-build expansion that will increase the facility's treatment capacity from 6 million gallons per day (MGD) to 12 MGD. This milestone marks a major investment in long-term, reliable surface water supplies for the Baytown area.

Through a progressive design-build delivery, Baytown Waterworks Partners has collaborated closely with Baytown Area Water Authority, in conjunction with BGE Engineering who serves as the Owner's Advisor, from early design and will continue to collaborate through construction to advance technical solutions, manage risk, and efficiently deliver the expanded facilities. The plant expansion is expected to be fully functional at target capacity by summer 2028.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. SERVICE AFTER SERVICE: VETERANS BUILDING HOUSTON'S FUTURE WITH MCCARTHY

When sailors and Marines arrived in Houston for its first-ever hosted Fleet Week, most people looked up - to the ships, the helicopters and the flyovers. At McCarthy, a lot of our partners look out - to the wharves, docks and water infrastructure they now help build along the same channels those vessels call home.

For veterans like Andy Garcia, Corey Everitte and John Walker, Fleet Week isn't just a celebration of military service. It's a reminder that their mission hasn't ended with military retirement; it has just evolved to helping build and protect the infrastructure that keeps a maritime city moving.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. TAKING PRIDE IN SAFETY: A CONVERSATION WITH BILLY NAYLOR

Having the privilege of leading national safety for McCarthy Building Companies, Inc. is one of the greatest honors of my life. It's something I'm extraordinarily proud of and something I never thought I'd accomplish. I want every employee to know there is not a day I take it for granted, not a day I don't take it seriously and not a day I'm not pulling hard for everyone to return home safely. Q: Serious Injury and Fatality (SIF) prevention is one of McCarthy's highest safety priorities for 2026. What makes SIF events different from other incidents, and what specific steps is McCarthy's national safety team taking this year to get in front of them before they happen?

A: SIF requires a mindset shift. That's the most important thing to understand about it.

For years, the construction industry was wired to think about safety through the lens of recordability. Our recordable rate is something we're mandated to submit on every proposal, every owner deliverable. And companies - including ours - have gotten very good at driving that number down. When I joined McCarthy in 2006, our rates were much higher. Reducing recordable incidents became the driver of our behaviors and the primary way we measured ourselves.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

value.Title: AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. - UP TO DATE DEVELOPMENTS WITH KIEWIT CORPORATION AND OTHERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK2-X8V3-S5B7-T3BS-00000-00AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. - UP TO DATE DEVELOPMENTS WITH KIEWIT CORPORATION AND OTHERS2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:13:28ZLexisNexisSTOCKAUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. - UP TO DATE DEVELOPMENTS WITH KIEWIT CORPORATION AND OTHERS

DASHBOARD: AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC.

PermID4296692948Websitehttp://www.austin-ind.comIndustryConstructionAddress3535 Travis Street Suite 300 Dallas, TX 75204

ACTIVITIES:

Austin Industries, Inc., founded in 1918, is a privately-owned construction firm headquartered in Dallas, Texas. Employing over 7,000 people, it operates through subsidiaries like Austin Commercial and Austin Bridge & Road, serving commercial, infrastructure, and industrial sectors. With annual revenues exceeding $2 billion, Austin delivers projects like airports, highways, and corporate campuses. In 2024, it expanded its portfolio with sustainable infrastructure projects, including renewable energy facilities. Its employee-owned culture fosters accountability, safety, and collaboration, with robust training programs. Competing with Balfour Beatty, Austin stands out for its regional expertise and commitment to quality. Recent initiatives include adopting advanced construction technologies like BIM and focusing on green building certifications, aligning with industry sustainability trends.

+ AUSTIN INDUSTRIES, INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ AUSTIN NAMED NO. 1 SCIENCE + TECHNOLOGY CONSTRUCTION FIRM

DALLAS - Austin is honored to once again be named the No. 1 Science + Technology Facility Construction Firm in the U.S. by World of Construction.

‍

From semiconductor fabs to digital infrastructure, our teams pour their skill, care, and dedication into creating the specialized spaces that help fuel tomorrow's breakthroughs. It's thanks to our employee‑owners, partners, and clients that this work is possible, and we're grateful for the trust they place in us every day.

‍

Firms recognized by World of Construction are those that are leading the way in delivering some of the most advanced science and technology facilities in the world. Austin is grateful for the recognition and proud to be leading our industry.

Source: Company Website

+ AUSTIN EARNS MULTIPLE HONORS AT ASA HOUSTON EVENT

HOUSTON - Austin Commercial was honored to be finalists for multiple Excellence in Construction Awards from American Subcontractors Association-Houston Chapter. Austin's Houston office was awarded the GC Safety Award and the Project of the Year Over $50M for the UH Memorial Hermann Football Operations Center.

‍

The company extends its thanks to our trade partners and employee-owners for all that you do, and for making these honors possible.

Source: Company Website

+ AUSTIN RANKED #27 ON BD+C'S TOP 100 HOSPITAL FACILITY CONSTRUCTION FIRMS

DALLAS - Austin has been named No. 27 on Building Design + Construction's (BD+C) 2025 Top 100 Hospital Facility Construction Firms list, reaffirming the company's long-standing position as a trusted builder in the healthcare market, and highlighting continued growth in this sector.

Austin has appeared on BD+C's healthcare rankings for years, but recent performance reflects a notable expansion in both volume and opportunity. That momentum is already visible in the work underway. In 2025, Austin began preconstruction on several major healthcare projects, including JPS Health Network Hospital Addition, The University of Texas at Austin & The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center Medical Complex Project (MCP), and The University of Texas MD Anderson Cancer Center Patient Care Building 1 (1PC).

This ranking is a testament to the expertise and dedication of Austin's employee-owners and highlights the importance of continued investment in people and resources as healthcare remains a key growth sector for the company.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 Press Releases]

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION PROTECTING WHAT LIES BELOW

Near the base of Nch'kay', or Mount Garibaldi, the Squamish community, in western Canada, has lived for decades with the threat of large debris flows. These fast-moving surges of mud, water, trees and boulders can cascade down the river with little warning. The Cheekye River, whose name comes from the Skwxwu7mesh (Squamish Nation language) word for "muddy waters," has a long history of these events.

Because of the historical risk, development on the alluvial fan - the broad deposit of sediment formed where the river spreads out at the base of the mountain - had been restricted for years. Despite many existing homes and businesses on the fan being in harm's way, little had been done to reduce the risk. Sqomish Sea to Sky Developments, a partnership between the Squamish Nation and local land development company Matthews West, owns just over 500 acres of this land.

Source: Company Website

+ KIEWIT'S 85 YEARS IN CANADA

In 2026, Kiewit proudly marks its 85th year of building in Canada. The company's Canadian story began in 1941 in the North, supporting construction of the Norman Wells Oil Refinery and Pipeline. In 1949, Kiewit opened its first Canadian office in Vancouver, establishing a permanent presence in the country. From those early roots, the company expanded across regions and markets, building the relationships, workforce and operational capacity required to serve clients coast to coast to coast. For the past 85 years, Kiewit has delivered complex projects across transportation, energy, marine, water, industrial and emerging sectors that support a changing economy.

"Diversifying our markets has made us a stronger, more resilient business," said Ryan Tones, president of Peter Kiewit Sons ULC. "Our teams have built hydroelectric facilities, ports and marine infrastructure, highways and transit systems, pipelines and power facilities, and essential water and wastewater systems that protect communities and natural resources. Our investment in our Marine Yard on the West Coast and our continued growth in water, wastewater and mining, reflect our commitment to serving Canada's evolving infrastructure needs and creating long-term opportunities for our people and partners."

Today, Kiewit employs more than 4,000 people across Canada, including a strong and growing craft workforce. Positioned across the country, these teams are building on decades of experience while preparing for Canada's next chapter.

The following timeline highlights select milestones and projects from the past 85 years and illustrates Kiewit's enduring role in advancing Canada's energy, infrastructure and economic development.

Source: Company Website

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION TIME CAPSULE: RECORD-BREAKING BRIDGES

From bustling city centers to remote wilderness areas, bridges stand as testaments to human ingenuity and the relentless pursuit of connection. Over the years, Kiewit has contributed to several record-breaking bridges unlike any before their time. This collection of Kieways articles revisits those milestone projects.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. [4 Press Releases]

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. MORE THAN 160 YEARS OF BUILDING FORWARD

For more than 160 years, McCarthy has been shaped by people who came before, building a legacy grounded in craftsmanship, integrity and long-term vision. This second edition of the company's history book captures the evolution from its earliest roots to a modern, employee-owned organization committed to innovation, partnership and great experiences for everyone. Along the way, it traces the milestones, bold decisions and defining projects that helped establish McCarthy as one of the industry's most respected builders. It is both a reflection on where McCarthy has been and a celebration of the ownership mindset shaping where it goes next.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. BAYTOWN AREA WATER AUTHORITY AND BAYTOWN WATERWORKS PARTNERS BREAK GROUND ON EAST SURFACE WATER TREATMENT PLANT PHASE II

Baytown Area Water Authority and Baytown Waterworks Partners, a joint venture between McCarthy Building Companies, Inc. and Carollo Engineers, are pleased to announce they have broken ground on Phase II of the Baytown Area Water Authority East Surface Water Treatment Plant, a progressive design-build expansion that will increase the facility's treatment capacity from 6 million gallons per day (MGD) to 12 MGD. This milestone marks a major investment in long-term, reliable surface water supplies for the Baytown area.

Through a progressive design-build delivery, Baytown Waterworks Partners has collaborated closely with Baytown Area Water Authority, in conjunction with BGE Engineering who serves as the Owner's Advisor, from early design and will continue to collaborate through construction to advance technical solutions, manage risk, and efficiently deliver the expanded facilities. The plant expansion is expected to be fully functional at target capacity by summer 2028.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. SERVICE AFTER SERVICE: VETERANS BUILDING HOUSTON'S FUTURE WITH MCCARTHY

When sailors and Marines arrived in Houston for its first-ever hosted Fleet Week, most people looked up - to the ships, the helicopters and the flyovers. At McCarthy, a lot of our partners look out - to the wharves, docks and water infrastructure they now help build along the same channels those vessels call home.

For veterans like Andy Garcia, Corey Everitte and John Walker, Fleet Week isn't just a celebration of military service. It's a reminder that their mission hasn't ended with military retirement; it has just evolved to helping build and protect the infrastructure that keeps a maritime city moving.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. TAKING PRIDE IN SAFETY: A CONVERSATION WITH BILLY NAYLOR

Having the privilege of leading national safety for McCarthy Building Companies, Inc. is one of the greatest honors of my life. It's something I'm extraordinarily proud of and something I never thought I'd accomplish. I want every employee to know there is not a day I take it for granted, not a day I don't take it seriously and not a day I'm not pulling hard for everyone to return home safely. Q: Serious Injury and Fatality (SIF) prevention is one of McCarthy's highest safety priorities for 2026. What makes SIF events different from other incidents, and what specific steps is McCarthy's national safety team taking this year to get in front of them before they happen?

A: SIF requires a mindset shift. That's the most important thing to understand about it.

For years, the construction industry was wired to think about safety through the lens of recordability. Our recordable rate is something we're mandated to submit on every proposal, every owner deliverable. And companies - including ours - have gotten very good at driving that number down. When I joined McCarthy in 2006, our rates were much higher. Reducing recordable incidents became the driver of our behaviors and the primary way we measured ourselves.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
